name: Audit Dependencies with cargo-audit and cargo-deny
description: Scan Rust dependencies for security vulnerabilities, license violations, and supply chain risks.
trendingReason: Supply chain security is a top priority in modern software development

steps.title: Install cargo-audit
steps.description: Install the security audit tool that checks against the RustSec advisory database.

steps.title: Run a vulnerability scan
steps.description: Check all dependencies for known security vulnerabilities.
steps.tip: Run this in CI to catch new vulnerabilities as the advisory database is updated daily.

steps.title: Install and configure cargo-deny
steps.description: Set up cargo-deny for comprehensive dependency policy enforcement.
steps.command: cargo install cargo-deny && cargo deny init

steps.title: Check licenses and advisories
steps.description: Run cargo-deny to check for license compliance and security advisories.
steps.command: cargo deny check advisories && cargo deny check licenses

steps.title: Check for duplicate and banned dependencies
steps.description: Detect duplicate crate versions and enforce banned crate rules.
steps.command: cargo deny check bans && cargo deny check sources
steps.tip: Duplicate dependencies increase binary size. Use 'cargo deny check bans' to find them.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Rust Dependency Audit
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Security Vulnerabilities ==="
cargo audit 2>&1 || true

echo ""
echo "=== Dependency Policy Check ==="
if command -v cargo-deny &> /dev/null; then
  echo "--- Advisories ---"
  cargo deny check advisories 2>&1 || true
  echo ""
  echo "--- Licenses ---"
  cargo deny check licenses 2>&1 || true
  echo ""
  echo "--- Bans (duplicates) ---"
  cargo deny check bans 2>&1 || true
else
  echo "cargo-deny not installed. Run: cargo install cargo-deny"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Audit Commands ==="
echo "Vulnerabilities: cargo audit"
echo "Fix vuln:        cargo audit fix"
echo "Advisories:      cargo deny check advisories"
echo "Licenses:        cargo deny check licenses"
echo "Duplicates:      cargo deny check bans"
echo "All checks:      cargo deny check"

faq.question: What is the difference between cargo-audit and cargo-deny?
faq.answer: cargo-audit focuses on security vulnerabilities from the RustSec database. cargo-deny covers a broader scope including license compliance, banned crates, duplicate dependencies, and source restrictions.

faq.question: How do I allow a known vulnerability temporarily?
faq.answer: In cargo-deny's deny.toml, add the advisory ID to the [advisories] ignore list with a reason and expiry date. For cargo-audit, use --ignore RUSTSEC-XXXX-XXXX.

faq.question: Should I run audits in CI?
faq.answer: Yes. Run 'cargo audit' and 'cargo deny check' in your CI pipeline. The RustSec database is updated frequently, so a dependency that was safe yesterday might have a new advisory today.
